route.name: Teanaway River Link Up
route.description: Public GPX Washington overnighter with forest roads, river corridors, crossings, and Roslyn access.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Washington overnighter with forest roads, river corridors, crossings, and Roslyn access.
route.bestSeason: June to September; July is a good fit with river-crossing and fire checks.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Washington overnighter with forest roads, river corridors, crossings, and Roslyn access.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Filter from crossings and camp-adjacent water sources.
resources.food: Roslyn has food and beer before/after; no robust mid-route resupply.
resources.camping: Free DNR camps at Indian Camp, Twenty Nine Pines, and Teanaway are core options; confirm current rules.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Check crossing depth close to departure.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Not gate-confirmed until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
No exact FCFS campsite availability can be proven for July 11-12.
DNR page has a seasonal campground reopen alert and a Labor Mountain Fire closure alert; current open status and closure geometry must be refreshed.
Route-specific legality is not fully proven for the faint trail/river crossing/abandoned roadbed and any USFS-adjacent segments.
No official potable-water source confirmed at DNR campgrounds; natural-water treatment is mandatory.
Final burn restriction, smoke, weather, and river-crossing checks are time-sensitive and cannot be cleared on July 2 for July 10-13.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh before next-weekend gate confirmation.
Confirm with WA DNR Southeast Region or current DNR recreation openings that 29 Pines, Teanaway Camping Area, and Indian Camp are open for July 10-13, 2026.
Overlay GPX against DNR Teanaway maps, private inholdings, and Labor Mountain Fire closure; verify every road/trail/river-crossing segment is bicycle-legal.
Check WA DNR Burn Portal and local county restrictions on July 9-10.
Refresh AirNow/smoke and NWS point forecast within 24-48 hours of departure.
Check Teanaway river crossing depth/conditions close to departure and define a bail/reroute if unsafe.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Good July fit with river-crossing, DNR camp, fire, and smoke checks.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: 29 Pines, Teanaway Camping Area, and Indian Camp are FCFS/no-reservation DNR camps; exact July 11 availability cannot be guaranteed.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: DNR page flags Labor Mountain Fire burned-area closure until further notice and burn restrictions can change daily.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: DNR allows existing unmaintained trails and gated roads at own risk, but GPX includes a river crossing, faint trail/abandoned roadbed, possible USFS-adjacent segments, and potential Labor Mountain Fire closure intersection.

days.name: Teanaway River Link Up segment 1
days.camp: Free DNR camps at Indian Camp, Twenty Nine Pines, and Teanaway are core options; confirm current rules.

logistics: Final gate confirmation requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.
